name: Simulation success - process result with points, badges, games, vouchers
description: Documented Loyalty Engine Realtime Output example (response body for POST). status=true, processResult populated with member points balance and per-journal results (badges, point changes, games, voucher). Mirrors doc JSON example verbatim. Note: per doc, processResult collection is empty when a simulation is actually run and simulationResults is populated instead; the doc's printed example nonetheless shows processResult populated and simulationResults=null, reproduced here as-is.
notes: VERIFIED against doc. Resource path (line 21978): /connect/realtime/loyalty/programs/${programName}; full URL example (21982-21983) includes /services/data/vXX.X prefix and uses program name "StarAir_Program". HTTP method POST only (21990). Available version 55.0 (21986); individual properties have their own versions (shouldCheckCouponUsageLimit=62.0, transactionJournals=54.0, runSetting=55.0). Request root XML tag <loyaltyEngineInput> (21993). Request property table at 22104-22117. Response schema named "Loyalty Engine Realtime Output" in the resource (line 22120) but the Response Bodies section heading is "Loyalty Engine Realtime" (line 29822) — appears truncated; same schema. CAVEATS: (1) Doc behavior note — when a simulation actually runs, processResult is empty and simulationResults (Transaction Journal Map Output) is populated; the doc's printed JSON example contradicts this by showing processResult populated and simulationResults=null. I reproduced the doc example as-is for fixture fidelity. (2) simulationResults schema (Transaction Journal Map Output) has no JSON example printed inline here, so no simulation-populated fixture was built — would require fabricating fields. (3) No error/failure JSON example is shown in the doc (status=false / message populated is described in property table but not exemplified), so only a success fixture was created. (4) ${programName} is a path placeholder for the loyalty program's name (e.g. StarAir_Program, NTO). (5) Doc references "Interchangeable Request Fields" — some request field names are interchangeable (not enumerated here). (6) appliedPromotions array must be included per-journal to enforce promotion limits (note at 22008-22011).
